methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Scout AI is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis that has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies across the Defense & Security sector, including 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Anduril Industries, Shield AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety, supplemented by a 5-company peer cohort of same-sector and same/adjacent-stage benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage, using the $15M raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $38M. The Peer-Median method examines 5 comparable companies—including Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed stage), and four Series A firms (Ares Industries at $150M, NODA AI at $125M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Stark Defence at $500M)—producing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $60M to $500M. The two-method triangulation yields a low of $32M, median of $94M, and high of $172M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $172M, with a central estimate near $94M. Key uncertainties include how closely the peer set reflects Scout AI's product maturity, go-to-market traction, and defensibility versus the broader cohort of defense-tech entrants.
